channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: G.I. Joe HeroScape First Wave Expansions Review - Calling Reinforcements
item.content:encoded:
What makes the G.I. Joe franchise so special to me is the absolutely huge cast of memorable characters - good and bad - who’ve made their way into the imagination of millions. As G.I. Joe makes its grand entrance into the wargaming world of HeroScape, it only makes sense that a first wave of supporting characters should release alongside the G.I. Joe HeroScape: Rumble At The Rift Battle Box. I got a chance to check out the new G.I. Joe HeroScape Army Expansions, so read on for my thoughts!

G.I. Joe Greenshirts and Battle Copter Army Expansion

First up is a squad and flying unit combo to use to fill out your forces, featuring the famously unnamed Greenshirts and Battle Copter.

G.I. Joe - Greenshirts

The Greenshirts are a squad of four figures in one unit, which allow for a lot of flexibility and maneuverability on the battlefield as you can spread them around to plink at your foes.

Their stats, as with all squads, aren’t amazing - but their special ability “Wherever There’s Trouble” is extremely interesting. Before you take a turn with the Greenshirts, you can move up to two unengaged G.I. Joe or Soldier Heroes you control up to two spaces.

Notice there’s no range to this, and you don’t need to have line of sight or anything like that. You just get to move them, no matter where they are on the battlefield! Honestly, the more I think about it, the more this (kind of hilariously) mirrors the role of Greenshirts in the franchise - they get in there and take the heat while the heroes get to move into position for that lethal blow!

G.I. Joe - Battle Copter

There’s so much benefit to running some units with Flying in your army, and the Battle Copter is here to deliver the pain to your enemies. It has the “Battle Ready” special ability, which allows you to swap its place with another unit.

But the “Strafe Special Attack” is really where this Copter shines, as it attacks in a Line 5, hitting everyone except friendly figures with three attack dice! Not too shabby for the ol’ Battle Copter.

COBRA Troopers and COBRA Flight Pod Army Expansion

Mirroring the Greenshirts and Copter set, we also get a squad and aerial unit for COBRA.

COBRA - COBRA Troopers

The COBRA Troopers are all about working as a lethal team, and setting up the big bass to do their thing. Before you take a turn with them, you can first take a turn with a Warlord or Profiteer Hero you control.

And their “Coils of Cobra” special ability tightens around your enemies as each member of the squad of four add 1 to their attack value for each adjacent unique COBRA figure you control, meaning if you pair these guys up with named characters on your board, you’ll be boosting their (measly) 1 attack.

They’ll certainly take some work to play around and optimize, but I could see some nasty lists getting built around buffing up what should be very weak squad members.

COBRA - COBRA Flight Pod

Like the Battle Copter, the Flight Pod also has Flying and “Battle Ready,” but I like the way these two are differentiated. Whereas the Copter attacks in Line 5 and doesn’t incite friendly fire, the Flight Pod… doesn’t really care about friendly fire.

All’s fair in COBRA war, right? That’s why their “Air-To-Ground Missile Special Attack” is so nasty, with a Range of 4 and Explosion 1, it’s going to dish out damage to anyone who gets in its way. Philosophically, this makes so much sense, and so clearly delineates between COBRA and the Joes.

Also, it should be said, the Flight Pod might be the coolest-looking figure in these sets. I love the clear plastic glass dome covering the hull of the Pod.

Strike Forces Army Expansion

Now we’re getting into it! This expansion comes with two COBRA and two G.I. Joe units to help augment your armies with a few familiar faces!

G.I. Joe - Lady Jaye

Lady Jaye, the master of disguise, adds a lot of mobility to the battlefield with her “Motorized Zipline” special ability. You start the game with 3 hipline glyphs on her card, and can place them around the map to allow any small or medium figure to zip between the spaces.

With her 6 Move stat, this means she can get out into the battlefield first, start laying down Ziplines, and all of a sudden your slower characters that hit harder and whipping around the battlefield.

Her “Master of Disguise 14” special ability makes her harder to target by Squad figures, so hopefully she won’t get pecked to death by lesser grunts on the battlefield.

G.I. Joe - Jinx And Kamakura

A unique squad of two, the Ninjas Jinx and Kamakura add some trickery and flexibility to your Joes. To start, you can let another Ninja hero you control go first before activating them thanks to their “Learn From The Master” Special Ability.

You also gain 1 additional attack die for each other friendly Ninja adjacent to the target, and they have the same “Phantom Walk” ability as Snake Eyes and Storm Shadow. Though Snake Eyes really wants to be alone to get the most out of his special abilities, these two alone can dish out some real damage with their Range 4 when coordinating together.

COBRA - Baroness

The mean queen herself, Baroness makes her entry into HeroScape in this Strike Forces Army Expansion. The brains behind a lot of COBRA’s most dastardly machinations, her “Intelligence Chief” special ability lets you take two turns with different COBRA or Agent heroes you control.

She can also interfere with your enemy’s Order Markers, potentially (with a high roll) moving them around - completely messing up your opponent’s flow.

Add to that the fact that she never takes leaving engagement attacks, has a huge Range 7, and rolls 4 attack dice - she’s truly a danger on the battlefield.

COBRA - Crimson Twins

Everyone’s favorite dynamic duo, the Crimson Twins Tomax and Xamot are - just like Jinx and Kamakura - a Unique Squad of two. But unlike those two, their health pool is shared between the both of them.

They also have some serious list-building implications, as their “Bankroll” special ability says you can turn one Unique Hero into a COBRA hero, regardless of what it says on that figure’s card. Want to combo off of other COBRA figures who care about affiliation? Use the Crimson Twins to recruit someone like Snake Eyes (he would never!).

G.I. Joe HeroScape - Serpentor Army Expansion

Finally, let’s check out Serpentor, a single unique Army Expansion unit for G.I. Joe HeroScape. Currently available as a free bonus when purchasing the Rumble At The Rift Battle Box, Serpentor enters the fray as one of the nastiest figures in the line - a perfect match for his deadly presence in the franchise.

So what does Serpentor do on the field? He attacks, up close and personal, and generally wants to be left alone. He has a double attack, but also subtracts 1 from the defense value of all figures adjacent to him (including your own friendly figures).

Finally, his “I’ll Do It Myself” special ability perfectly captures his impatience and arrogance, allowing you to move an order marker from another friendly figure onto his card and activate him instead.

G.I. Joe HeroScape First Wave Expansions - Final Thoughts

Just like with the core set, this first wave of Army Expansions perfectly captures the feeling of each of these characters. The sculpts for each of the models are vibrant and evocative, and the only complaint I can muster here is the inclusion of certain characters over others.

But each of these models, just like the Rumble core set, gently pushes at the boundaries of the HeroScape ruleset while keeping the rules as light and airy as we’ve come to expect.

The copies of these G.I. Joe HeroScape army expansions used in this review were provided by Renegade Game Studios.

Giaco Furino joined the TechRaptor team as a Staff Writer in 2019 after searching for a dedicated place to write and talk about Tabletop Games. In 2020, he… More about Giaco



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/reviews/gi-joe-heroscape-first-wave-expansions-review-calling-reinforcements
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Video Rental Store Sim Retro Rewind Racks Up 100k Sales, Reveals Roadmap
item.content:encoded:
Blood Pact Studios, developer and publisher of nostalgic video rental store sim Retro Rewind, has revealed that its game has crossed a major sales milestone, as well as unveiling its very first roadmap.

In a Steam post, Blood Pact says that Retro Rewind has managed to sell 100,000 copies. The post was uploaded on Sunday morning (or Saturday evening depending on your time zone), so that sales figure was reached in just a few days.

Blood Pact says it's blown away by the support players have shown to Retro Rewind, and that as a team of just two developers, said support has "made [their] dream a reality" after almost two years' work on the project.

In addition to revealing the sales figure, Blood Pact also unveils the first Retro Rewind roadmap (now that's a pleasing phrase), and the two-person studio is planning lots of new stuff for the game.

Features like backroom storage, a VHS repair station, and controller integration have all been promised, as well as the ability to rent out video games. Extra decorations, music, and other unlocks have also been teased.

All of these updates will remain free; Blood Pact says that although Retro Rewind is a full release rather than an Early Access game, there are "no plans to charge for future content", with the goal simply being to "make [the game] the best video store experience possible for all players".

Retro Rewind is part of a burgeoning work sim genre that also includes luminaries like PowerWash Simulator 2, and it's arguably also spiritually akin to indies like Moonlighter and its sequel. It's clear that Blood Pact's game has managed to find its niche, at any rate.

Retro Rewind is available right now on PC via Steam, and if you pick it up, there's plenty of new content to come as well. Stay tuned for more!

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/retro-rewind-sales-100k-roadmap
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: G.I. Joe HeroScape - Rumble At The Rift Review - Now You Know
item.content:encoded:
Yo Joe! Why in the world is the G.I. Joe team clashing against deadly COBRA forces in the mystical realm of Valhalla? Gotta be Dr. Mindbender’s fault, right? Renegade Game Studios sent along the brand new G.I. Joe HeroScape Rumble At The Rift Battle Box set for me to review. Does this certified Joe-freak love it?  Read on to find out!

I also got a chance to check out the first wave of new units for G.I. Joe HeroScape not included in this battle box!

G.I. Joe HeroScape: Rumble At The Rift is a new set for the incredible HeroScape (which we’ve previously reviewed here). A skirmish war-game pitting the Joes against COBRA, it has everything two players need to jump into the game. The set, and all additional units, come painted or un-painted, with the un-painted versions being slightly less expensive for the budget conscious.

Box Contents

The game contains 8 fully assembled miniatures (either un- or pre-painted, depending on the set you choose), 29 hex tile terrain pieces, 2 COBRA fortification pieces, 8 combat dice, 1 20-sided die, markers, army cards, a core rulebook for the game, and a scenario guide containing multiple different scenarios to run through.

What’s New?

Along with eight new miniatures for the game - which we’ll dive into in great detail below - the game also introduces new runes which switch up gameplay, and three new types of tiles.

Shadow tiles cloak your miniatures in darkness, adding +1 defense to small, medium, and large miniatures. Conversely, there are the incredibly radical glow-in-the-dark toxic land and toxic water tiles, which give -1 and -2 to defense if you’re standing in them, respectively.

This sort of sums up exactly what I love so much about HeroScape - they introduce an entirely new type of tile like the toxic water that looks amazing, and its impact on the game is huge but simple. Incredible design choice here!

G.I. Joe HeroScape: Rumble At The Rift Characters

Now let’s jetpack jump into the eight different, totally iconic characters included in this set. For long-time Joe fans, it’s hard to think of many other iconic classics that should have made the top eight (and surprise! Some of them are included in the other sets).

G.I. Joe - Duke

The sometimes-leader of the Joes, Duke enters the fray with a miniature that perfectly captures his essence. And he is, without a doubt, one of the best leaders in the game.

Not only can you use the reveal of his order number to instead activate two other Heroes, but as long as his order marker has been revealed other G.I. Joe and Soldier figures that are nearby and can see him add 1 to their attack value. Yo, Joe, indeed!

G.I. Joe - Scarlett

My main gal! Scarlett is all about intel gathering, allowing you to look at enemy order markers and adjust your own. Along with that, an enemy she attacks lowers their Defense value by 1 for each revealed Order Marker on its Army Card. That’s a cool ability we don’t get to see too often!

To add to all that, she also gets to move up to 2 spaces and make 1 normal attack against an adjacent figure. This really does a good job of summarizing Scarlett - she gets the intel and uses it with lethal efficiency.

G.I. Joe - Roadblock

I’ll never forget the first time the hulking Roadblock appeared in the comics, lugging around one of the biggest guns I’ve ever seen. His special ability, “Machine Gun Special Attack,” allows him to lay down a torrent of fire against multiple enemies within range (Range 5).

Straightforward, effective, and powerful - that’s Roadblock for ya.

G.I. Joe - Snake Eyes

Of course a classic Joe lineup isn’t complete without Snake Eyes, the silent assassin with enough trauma to power a VAMP. Snake Eyes is a melee character with a Range Value of 1, but he’s fast (he and Scarlett both have an impressive Move: 6).

As the assassin of the group (because every group of good guys needs an assassin!) he hits harder when attacking an enemy figure not adjacent to any other enemy figures, has better evasion when someone’s trying to target him out in the open, and can move through all figures’ spaces without triggering leaving engagement attacks. Stealthy, silent, deadly, and cool as hell.

COBRA - COBRA Commander

Moving onto the baddies, COBRA Commander leads the charge - though, like the Commander we know and love, he’s not much of a leader. While Duke’s abilities are all about getting the most out of the team, COBRA Commander is a lethal and slippery force on his own.

First up, his Cutlassss of COBRA means he auto-adds a successful hit to each attack against adjacent figures, and his hilariously named “I’ll Get You Next Time” special ability states that after he receives 1 or more wounds from a normal attack you can move him up to 2 spaces without suffering leaving engagement attacks.

COBRA - Destro

Chrome Dome himself, Destro enters the fray with loads of gadgets and tech. At the start of the game, you can equip two different unique heroes in your squad with Tech Glyphs, which do everything from adding damage to giving temporary flight!

His Beryllium Steel Mask gives him 1 auto-shield when defending against ranged attacks, and he can always take another attack after making his first attack. He’s got gadgets, he runs guns, this figure is the Destro we know and love.

COBRA - Doctor Mindbender

Look, he’s no Dr. Venom (real Joe heads know), but Doctor Mindbender is the one who, through hasty experiments, got the Joes and COBRA into this mess in the first place! His abilities play with people’s minds, as you’d expect, allowing you to move friendly units on his activation.

His special ability “Bend Mind 13” which says - with a roll of 13 or higher - you actually get to take temporary control of an enemy Army card and activate it! That’s pretty unprecedented in the game, and perfectly speaks to Dr. Mindbender’s ability to truly be bending those minds.

COBRA - Storm Shadow

Finally, it’s everyone’s other favorite assassin - Storm Shadow! He throws down smoke grenades placing those shadow tiles I spoke about earlier and can - like Snake Eyes - move through characters without provoking attacks.

But his real specialty, which I find very useful on the battlefield, is his “Whirlwind Assault” - Storm Shadow may attack any number of times during his turn, but can’t attack any single figure more than once. This feels so Storm Shadow - while Snake Eyes gets in, kills, and gets out, Storm Shadow throws himself into the middle of the chaos and just starts slicing.

G.I. Joe HeroScape - Rumble At The Rift: Final Thoughts

G.I. Joe HeroScape - Rumble At The Rift is, without a doubt, one of my favorite gaming experiences of the year. As a G.I. Joe fan (especially of the incredible Larry Hama Marvel comics), I was ready to either love this or bounce off it (if it was a logo-slap).

But this game was made by people who clearly also love G.I. Joe, and understand what makes each of these iconic characters so unique. Snake Eyes and Storm Shadow, for example, feel both mechanically similar and wonderfully unique, matching the character personas perfectly.

The mechanics push the design of HeroScape even further without overcomplicating things, and the sculpts for all of the figures look great. My only complaint is that I want more now! I’m ready for Stalker, Major Bludd, Zartan, and Gung-ho! Let’s go, Renegade Joes!

The copy of G.I. Joe HeroScape - Rumble At The Rift used in the creation of this review was provided by Renegade Game Studios.

Giaco Furino joined the TechRaptor team as a Staff Writer in 2019 after searching for a dedicated place to write and talk about Tabletop Games. In 2020, he… More about Giaco



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/reviews/gi-joe-heroscape-rumble-at-rift-review-now-you-know
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Xbox Partner Preview for March 2026 Will Feature Stranger Than Heaven and More
item.content:encoded:
Xbox has announced a new Partner Preview showcase for this week, and it's set to include info on upcoming games like Ryu Ga Gotoku's Stranger Than Heaven, as well as brand new reveals.

As outlined in a new Xbox Wire blog post, the showcase is happening this Thursday, March 26th. It'll air at 1pm Eastern, which works out at 10am Pacific or 5pm GMT.

With regards to games, you can expect new info about Stranger Than Heaven, which will hopefully be getting a release date during the show. Owlcat will also be showcasing its upcoming Mass Effect-esque action RPG The Expanse: Osiris Reborn.

As well as the aforementioned two games, Xbox is also promising an update on Stalker 2: Shadow of Chornobyl, although we don't know whether that update will constitute a free content drop along the lines of previous updates or a meatier paid expansion of some kind.

It's not just previously-revealed projects, though; Xbox also says you can expect "brand-new reveals, world premieres and Xbox Game Pass announcements", so even if none of the above games tickle your fancy, there's bound to be something that does.

What you shouldn't expect, however, is information about upcoming Xbox Game Studios titles. This is a Partner Preview, not a first-party Developer Direct, so it's all about Xbox's third-party offerings.

Along those same lines, don't expect anything on Xbox's recently-revealed Project Helix line of next-generation hardware. There's always someone who comes away from these presentations disappointed on that kind of score, so it's worth bringing up.

The next Xbox Partner Preview will be airing on Thursday, March 26th at 1pm Eastern/10am Pacific/5pm GMT (or your local equivalent, of course). You'll be able to check it out via the official Xbox YouTube and Twitch channels. Stay tuned for more on this and all other things Xbox.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/xbox-partner-preview-march-2026-stranger-than-heaven
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Capcom Says It Will Use Gen AI for "Efficiency" but Not for In-Game Assets
item.content:encoded:
Capcom has clarified its stance on generative AI, stating that while it does intend to use the technology during game production for "efficiency and productivity", it won't be generating in-game assets with AI.

As reported by Automaton Media, this information comes from a summary Capcom recently provided for an investor briefing session held earlier this year. In the session, Capcom says it "will not be implementing any AI-generated assets into [its] video game content".

However, the Resident Evil and Mega Man publisher does say it intends to "actively utilize" generative AI in order to improve efficiency and productivity during the development process, and that the "methods of usage" the studio is considering "includ[es] graphics, sound, and programming".

That's a slightly confusing message if you ask me; I'm not entirely sure what graphics and sound are if they're not "video game content", but perhaps Capcom means it wants to use the tech to generate placeholders or create guides.

Of course, that's an approach that isn't without controversy as well. Baldur's Gate 3 developer Larian Studios recently found itself in hot water among some fans when it admitted to using generative AI in what sounds like a similar way to Capcom's intended implementation.

Additionally, games like 11 bit studios' The Alters and recent release Crimson Desert have mistakenly (that's their story, anyway) been released with generative AI assets still intact, so even if the tech is used for placeholder purposes, that's no guarantee it won't bleed into the finished product.

Capcom isn't the first studio to embrace the use of generative AI, of course, and the tech appears to be growing more widespread in the gaming industry with each passing day. Still, there are some who vehemently oppose it, so the debate around whether AI is ethical or useful will continue to rage.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/capcom-gen-ai-stance
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
